name: Google Cloud (Vertex AI)
tagline: SAS wins on regulated industry depth, production governance, and business-user tooling beyond developer AI

overview.whatTheyDo: Google Cloud Platform offers Vertex AI (ML platform), BigQuery (data warehouse), Looker (BI), and Gemini (GenAI). Strong in AI/ML research heritage from Google Brain and DeepMind.
overview.sweetSpot: AI-forward organizations wanting cutting-edge ML capabilities. Companies already using BigQuery for analytics. Digital-native companies comfortable with Google Cloud ecosystem.
overview.typicalBuyer: CTO, VP Data/AI, Head of Data Engineering, Chief AI Officer
overview.phPresence: Growing PH presence with Google Cloud Philippines team. Less enterprise penetration than Azure/AWS in traditional industries but strong in digital-native companies and telcos.
overview.recentMomentum: Gemini model family, Vertex AI Agent Builder, BigQuery ML, Looker modernization. Strongest AI research brand leveraging DeepMind integration.
overview.pricingModel: Consumption-based. BigQuery (per-query pricing or flat rate), Vertex AI (compute + prediction endpoints). Committed use discounts available.

whereWeWin.headline: Regulated Industry Solutions
whereWeWin.explanation: SAS provides purpose-built fraud, AML, risk, and compliance solutions with regulatory expertise encoded. Google Cloud offers generic AI/ML building blocks — customers must build regulated industry solutions from scratch.
whereWeWin.proofPoint: SAS serves 9 of top 10 global banks for risk and compliance. Google Cloud has zero pre-built BSP-compliant fraud management solutions.

whereWeWin.headline: Production Model Governance
whereWeWin.explanation: SAS Model Manager provides formal governance workflows with approval chains, regulatory documentation, and audit trails. Vertex AI Model Registry tracks models but lacks formal regulated governance.
whereWeWin.proofPoint: BSP examiners look for documented model lifecycle with formal approval — SAS provides this natively; Vertex AI does not.

whereWeWin.headline: Business User Self-Service at Scale
whereWeWin.explanation: SAS enables non-technical users to build, validate, and deploy analytics through visual interfaces. Vertex AI is fundamentally developer/data scientist tooling. BigQuery ML adds SQL-based ML but limited for complex modeling.
whereWeWin.proofPoint: 60% of SAS users are business analysts. Vertex AI usage is 95%+ technical personas.

whereTheyWin.whyItMatters: Google has the strongest AI research brand (DeepMind, Gemini, TPUs). Attracts organizations wanting cutting-edge AI capabilities and access to latest models.
whereTheyWin.howToNeutralize: Research innovation ≠ production readiness for regulated industries. Ask: 'Which of these innovations has a BSP compliance framework?' SAS brings proven production deployment to regulated contexts.

whereTheyWin.whyItMatters: BigQuery ML allows data analysts to build ML models using SQL directly in the data warehouse. Low friction for existing BigQuery users.
whereTheyWin.howToNeutralize: BigQuery ML is limited to simple models and lacks governance. For anything beyond basic classification/regression, customers need a proper ML platform. SAS integrates with BigQuery as a data source.

whereTheyWin.whyItMatters: Gemini models are competitive with GPT-4. Vertex AI Agent Builder makes GenAI accessible. Strong vision/multimodal capabilities.
whereTheyWin.howToNeutralize: SAS governs GenAI outputs — bias detection, output monitoring, model cards. Position: use Gemini for GenAI experimentation, govern with SAS for production regulated use.

features.notes: Vertex Model Registry lacks formal regulated governance workflows
BigQuery ML is SQL-only; Vertex AI is developer-focused
Google leads in AI research (DeepMind, Gemini)
Gemini + Vertex AI Agent Builder is comprehensive
SAS sub-10ms scoring vs. custom Vertex endpoints
SAS dedicated forecasting vs. Vertex AI basic time series
SAS runs anywhere; GCP primarily cloud (Anthos for hybrid)
SAS has validated statistical procedures for regulated use
Both strong; SAS adds governance to AutoML output

landmines.question: Can you show me a pre-built fraud management system on GCP that meets BSP Circular 1213 requirements?
landmines.expectedAnswer: Nothing pre-built — would need to be custom-developed on Vertex AI
landmines.whatToSayNext: SAS Fraud Management is purpose-built for BSP 1213 compliance with pre-trained detection models for Philippine payment fraud patterns.

landmines.question: How would your business analysts — not data scientists — build and validate detection models?
landmines.expectedAnswer: Vertex AI requires Python/coding; BigQuery ML is limited to SQL-based models
landmines.whatToSayNext: SAS enables business users to build, validate, and monitor models visually. That's critical for scaling analytics beyond your data science team.

landmines.question: What's your model governance workflow for regulated industries — approval chains, documentation, audit trails?
landmines.expectedAnswer: Basic model registry without formal governance workflows
landmines.whatToSayNext: SAS Model Manager provides the formal governance workflow that BSP and IC examiners expect — approval chains, automated documentation, and full audit trails.

objections.objection: Google has the best AI — they invented transformers
objections.whyTheySayIt: Google's AI research brand is powerful. Executives associate Google with AI leadership.
objections.response: Google's AI research is world-class — that's not in dispute. The question for regulated industries is: can you put that AI into production with governance, compliance, and audit trails? SAS takes AI — from any source including Google's — and makes it production-ready for regulators.
proofPoints: Research AI ≠ production-governed AI in regulated industries
BSP doesn't care who invented the algorithm — they care about governance
objections.whatNotToSay: Don't argue Google's AI isn't good — it clearly is. Focus on the production/governance gap.

objections.objection: BigQuery ML lets our analysts do ML without SAS
objections.whyTheySayIt: BigQuery ML's SQL interface feels accessible. Seems like they don't need another platform.
objections.response: BigQuery ML is great for simple models on data already in BigQuery. For enterprise needs — complex model ensembles, network analytics, champion/challenger testing, regulatory governance, real-time scoring — you need a purpose-built ML platform. That's what SAS provides, and it connects to BigQuery as a data source.
proofPoints: BigQuery ML supports ~15 model types; SAS supports 200+
No governance or monitoring in BigQuery ML
objections.whatNotToSay: Don't dismiss BigQuery ML — it has value for simple use cases. Position SAS for the complex, regulated workloads.

phContext.localPresence: Growing Philippines presence. Google Cloud PH team expanding. Strong in digital-native companies and telcos. Less penetration in traditional banking/insurance vs. Azure/AWS.
phContext.localPricing: Consumption-based. Competitive with AWS. Can offer significant discounts for commitment. BigQuery pay-per-query model attractive for variable workloads.
regulatoryGaps: Model governance doesn't meet PH regulatory examination standards
Limited on-premises option for data sovereignty requirements

partnerWhen: Customer uses BigQuery as primary data warehouse — SAS connects natively
GenAI use cases where Gemini provides the model and SAS provides governance
Google Cloud team is supportive and not positioning Vertex AI as SAS replacement
Use case is analytics layer on top of BigQuery data
competeWhen: Google Cloud positioning Vertex AI as complete ML platform
Customer evaluating GCP for first time for analytics/ML
Budget is either/or with GCP committed spend
Primary use case is regulated analytics (fraud, AML, risk)

proofPoints.title: Different positioning in analyst reports
proofPoints.detail: Google Cloud leads in AI/ML innovation; SAS leads in governed analytics for regulated industries. Complementary not competitive in Gartner evaluations.

proofPoints.title: Build cost for fraud on GCP
proofPoints.detail: Building enterprise fraud management from Vertex AI components: 12-18 months, $3-5M in engineering. SAS Fraud Management deploys in 8-12 weeks with pre-built models.

proofPoints.title: SAS + BigQuery integration
proofPoints.detail: SAS Viya connects natively to BigQuery — customers use BigQuery for storage/querying and SAS for advanced analytics and governance.
